read_for: The shortest overview: decode, control, causal, trace, and failure load.
do_not_claim: A dashboard pass is not evidence for autonomous planning.

read_for: Backward-compatible dashboard name used as the main plot in the original handout.
do_not_claim: Dashboard aesthetics cannot upgrade weak controls.

read_for: Per-task residual probe versus surface baseline, and target-direction versus random-direction causal shifts.
do_not_claim: One or two wins imply real-world tool reliability.

read_for: Whether action-letter shifts grow with dose and whether target directions separate from random controls.
do_not_claim: A monotonic letter-prompt shift is open-ended competence.

read_for: Selected-depth context across decode, surface, and gap metrics.
do_not_claim: A bright layer is a full circuit.

read_for: The deterministic toy trace path from prompt to oracle tool to result to final answer.
Counterexamples and failure specimens sorted by severity.
do_not_claim: No automatic specimens means no failures exist.

read_for: Train/eval probe scores by residual depth with surface controls nearby.
do_not_claim: Depth trends identify a full tool-use circuit.

read_for: Required tool versus predicted tool at the selected depth.
do_not_claim: Toy-tool accuracy transfers to real agents.

read_for: Legacy name for the activation-addition dose-response curve.
do_not_claim: Letter-prompt shifts are open-ended tool competence.

read_for: Residual probe versus surface and shuffled controls.
do_not_claim: Probe accuracy is meaningful if surface cues match it.
Harness trace is a cognitive map.

read_for: Would corrupted tool results alter the toy final answer.
do_not_claim: The model would verify corrupted results.

read_for: Known-trace label scaffold and human-review requirement.
do_not_claim: The model knows why it used the tool.
